description: You are a teacher at an OLPC-equipped school preparing the end-of-quarter academic progress report. Student assessment records are at /home/ga/Documents/class_records.csv, a semicolon-delimited file with columns: StudentID, Name, Subject, Quarter, Score, Attendance, StudyHoursWeekly. Write a Python script /home/ga/Documents/class_analysis.py (standard library only) that parses the CSV, computes per-subject average score for each quarter (Q1-Q4), identifies at-risk students whose cross-subject average score dropped more than 10 points from Q1 to Q4 or whose overall attendance is below 75 percent, computes the Pearson correlation coefficient between StudyHoursWeekly and average Score, and generates /home/ga/Documents/class_dashboard.html containing a subject-by-quarter averages table, an at-risk students table with each student's name, Q1 average, Q4 average, and attendance highlighted in red, and the correlation coefficient. Open the HTML in Sugar's Browse activity to verify it renders correctly. Create a document in Sugar Write titled 'Q4 Progress Report' with a table listing each at-risk student's name, Q1 average, Q4 average, and attendance. Below the table, state the subject with the lowest Q4 average and the correlation value. Save the document to /home/ga/Documents/progress_report.odt.

metadata.data_source: Deterministically generated student performance records (25 students, 5 subjects, 4 quarters)
metadata.at_risk_criteria: cross-subject avg drop > 10 from Q1 to Q4 OR overall attendance < 75%
